position_type.brief: A simple data structure representing a position type with descriptive and abbreviated names.
position_type.details: The position_type struct serves as a container for identifying different types of positions within the system, holding both a full descriptive name ('name') and a short, abbreviated name ('short_name'). These members enable flexible display, comparison, and serialization of position types in various contexts such as user interfaces, reports, and data exchange. The design emphasizes simplicity and clarity, providing direct access to the name attributes for easy integration with other system components. This struct does not include methods, relying on external functions or operators for manipulation and comparison, and is intended to be used as a lightweight, descriptive identifier within larger data models.
position_type.notes: This struct is a plain data holder without member functions, emphasizing straightforward data storage. It is often used in conjunction with other classes or functions that handle logic or behavior related to position types.
position_type.rationale: The position_type struct exists to standardize the representation of position identifiers across the system, ensuring consistent naming and abbreviation conventions. Its simplicity allows it to be easily integrated into larger data structures or serialization processes, supporting system-wide clarity and maintainability.
position_type.name: position_type

name.brief: Holds the descriptive name of the position type.
name.details: The 'name' variable stores the human-readable identifier for a position type, utilizing the custom String class that offers enhanced string operations like case-insensitive comparisons. It is initialized during the creation of a position_type object and is used across the codebase for display purposes, comparisons, and identification of different position types. Its extended functionalities facilitate flexible and robust string handling related to position type names.
name.notes: Since String extends standard string capabilities, 'name' can be compared case-insensitively and manipulated with advanced methods, which can be useful for user interface display and logic checks.
name.rationale: This variable exists to provide a clear, flexible, and consistent way to identify position types by name, supporting features like user-friendly display and reliable comparisons regardless of case variations.
name.name: name

short_name.brief: A string that holds the abbreviated, human-readable name of the position type.
short_name.details: The `short_name` variable contains a concise label representing the position type, primarily used for display, logging, or user interface purposes. It is initialized with a descriptive short name and utilizes the custom `String` class, which offers enhanced string manipulation capabilities, including case-insensitive comparisons. This allows for flexible handling and comparison of position type names throughout the application, ensuring consistency and ease of use in various contexts.
short_name.notes: Since `short_name` is stored using the `String` class, it benefits from extended features such as case-insensitive operations, which can simplify name comparisons and searches within the codebase.
short_name.rationale: The variable exists to provide a standardized, easily accessible short label for position types, enabling clear identification and consistent presentation across different parts of the system.
short_name.name: short_name
